title: Henckelia stapfii

Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine, and research has confirmed its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Henckelia stapfii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Henckelia stapfii is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical treatment, and it may also be formulated into capsules or extracts for oral consumption.
